pi_items.source: Task description specifies '~/Documents/candy_problem.txt'; export_result.sh line 12 checks TEXT_FILE=/home/ga/Documents/candy_problem.txt; setup_task.sh creates /home/ga/Documents directory
Task description specifies '~/Documents/candy_success.png'; export_result.sh line 28 checks SCREENSHOT_FILE=/home/ga/Documents/candy_success.png; setup_task.sh creates /home/ga/Documents directory

pi_items.verified_value: The 'Share' activity exists in GCompris. It teaches division/distribution by having children share candies equally among other children. Activity was created by Stefan Toncu during Google Summer of Code.
pi_items.source: Web search results confirm: 'Share is a game which aims to teach children how to divide numbers in a funny way: by sharing candies to some boys and girls.' (stefantoncu29.wordpress.com); Additional references in GCompris news and documentation confirm activity exists.

pi_items.verified_value: The Share activity uses drag-and-drop mechanics to distribute candies. Users drag candies from a source pile to children's areas on screen to distribute equally.
pi_items.source: Web search result: 'The user has to drag the exact amount of candies from the left panel to the child friends' areas on the main screen.' This matches task description requirement: 'Distribute the candies equally by dragging them to the children'

pi_items.verified_value: Success is indicated when the source pile is empty and children are satisfied/happy, shown by success animation
pi_items.source: Task description: 'until the source pile is empty and the children are happy (success animation)'; Web search confirms Share activity shows success when distribution is complete

pi_items.verified_value: Format: 'Candies: X, Children: Y' (case-insensitive matching). Verifier.py line 61 regex: r"Candies:\s*(\d+).*Children:\s*(\d+)" confirms this format.
pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 60-65 specifies and validates the format; Task description requires: 'write the counts in the format: Candies: X, Children: Y'

pi_items.verified_value: GCompris is a real, publicly-available, open-source educational software suite maintained by KDE (GNU AGPL v3 licensed) with 100+ activities for children aged 2-10. The task uses real GCompris software.
pi_items.source: Web searches confirm GCompris is real software; gcompris.net is official site; GitHub repositories (github.com/KDE/gcompris) contain source code

pi_items.verified_value: The task is NOT based on external dataset. Instead, it uses the real, interactive GCompris application. Each task instance generates unique data (random number of candies and children) based on the Share activity's internal game logic.
pi_items.source: task.json hooks show pre_task/post_task scripts that launch GCompris; No external data sources or URLs in scripts; setup_task.sh line 23-24 launches GCompris application; Data generated by GCompris at runtime

pi_items.verified_value: Verifier checks: (1) text file exists and created during task, (2) screenshot exists and created during task, (3) text file matches format 'Candies: X, Children: Y', (4) VLM analysis of trajectory for activity correctness
pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 48-81: File-based verification checks file existence, creation timestamp, and format; VLM verification via query_vlm for trajectory analysis

pi_items.verified_value: timeout_sec=300 (5 minutes) and max_steps=60 are reasonable for: launching GCompris, navigating to Share activity in Math/Numeration category, counting candies and children, distributing them via drag-and-drop, achieving success state, creating files, and taking screenshot.
pi_items.source: Task workflow complexity: launch app, navigate menu, count visible objects, perform multiple drag operations, complete activity; 300 seconds provides sufficient time for these interactive steps

privileged_info_summary: The Share activity is a real GCompris feature that teaches division by having children distribute candies equally among other children using drag-and-drop mechanics. The task correctly specifies that the agent must create two files: candy_problem.txt at /home/ga/Documents/ with format 'Candies: X, Children: Y', and candy_success.png at /home/ga/Documents/ showing the successful completion screen. The activity uses GCompris's real interactive game engine where each instance generates unique data based on internal game logic. The verifier validates task completion through: (1) file existence and creation timestamps, (2) text format matching, (3) VLM analysis of the agent trajectory to confirm the Share activity was found and executed correctly.
